# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

interface Config {
  modulePath: string;
  moduleName: string;
}

interface PluginModule {
  default: (config: Config) => Promise<any>;
}

async function loadModule(path: string): Promise<PluginModule> {
  try {
    const module = await import(path);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to load module from ${path}: ${error}`);
  }
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const config: Config = {
    modulePath: url.searchParams.get('module') || Deno.env.get('MODULE_PATH') || '',
    moduleName: url.searchParams.get('name') || Deno.env.get('MODULE_NAME') || 'default'
  };

  if (!config.modulePath) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: 'Module path not provided' }),
      { status: 400, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }

  try {
    const module = await loadModule(config.modulePath);
    const result = await module.default(config);
    
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ success: true, data: result }),
      { status: 200, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }),
      { status: 500, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }
}

Deno.serve(handleRequest);
